channel.title: CPaaS Pricing & Policy Changes
channel.description: SMS API price changes and provider policy updates.

item.title: WhatsApp free service-message window ends Oct 1; all non-template replies billable
item.description: From October 1, 2026, Meta will charge for service messages—human-agent or third-party AI replies within the 24-hour customer service window—at the same per-message rate as utility and authentication templates in each country. Utility templates sent inside the 24-hour window, previously free since July 1, 2025, also become billable. No volume discounts apply to service messages. Exact market rates will be published by September 1, 2026.

item.title: WhatsApp Meta Business Agent messages billed at $2/M tokens from Aug 1
item.description: Starting August 1, 2026, Meta charges for messages handled by its own Meta Business Agent Platform on WhatsApp at $2.00 USD per 1 million tokens globally. One typical message consumes 20,000–25,000 tokens, equating to roughly 4–5 cents per message. This creates a new billing tier separate from human-agent service messages and standard template messages. No volume discounts apply to this tier.

item.title: WhatsApp API raises marketing rates in UK/Italy/Spain; auth/util surges in HK, SG
item.description: Effective July 1, 2026, Meta raised WhatsApp Business API marketing message rates 15â€“20% in the United Kingdom, Italy, and Spain. Authentication and utility rates increased approximately 130% in Hong Kong, 42% in Singapore, and 32â€“65% in Hungary, Romania, and Qatar. Poland was the sole market with decreases: marketing rates fell ~57% and auth/utility ~42%. These markets moved from regional pricing pools to standalone country-specific rate cards.

item.title: WhatsApp billing localized to BRL for Brazil from July 1; migration mandatory by Jun 2027
item.description: From July 1, 2026, eligible Brazil-based partners and directly-integrated clients must create new WhatsApp Business Accounts (WABAs) in Brazilian Reals (BRL), invoiced by Meta's local entity Facebook Brasil. Accounts created before July 1 must migrate to BRL by June 30, 2027 or face message delivery suspension. Currency Migration APIs became available June 1, 2026 to support the transition. Gradual rollout began July 1 as planned.

item.title: Twilio requires privacy policy and T&C URLs for new A2P 10DLC campaign registrations
item.description: Starting June 30, 2026, Twilio's Messaging REST API rejects new A2P 10DLC campaign registrations that omit PrivacyPolicyUrl or TermsAndConditionsUrl fields. Both must be valid, publicly accessible URLs. Only new campaign submissions are affected; all previously registered campaigns continue unchanged. The change applies to POST /v1/Services/{MessagingServiceSid}/Compliance/Usa2p, and Twilio notes campaigns including these URLs pass carrier review at higher first-submission rates.

item.title: Meta Business Agent launches globally on WhatsApp for all business sizes
item.description: Meta announced and rolled out Meta Business Agent globally on June 3, 2026 at its Conversations conference in London, with over one million businesses already active at launch. The AI agent handles customer queries, product recommendations, appointment booking, and lead qualification 24/7 in local languages across WhatsApp, Messenger, and Instagram. The platform is free to access until paid billing begins August 1, 2026 at $2 per million tokens. Meta plans tiered subscription offerings for businesses of varying sizes in coming months.

item.title: WhatsApp API: Pakistan auth/util +85%, Turkey -83%, Saudi Arabia/India adjusted Apr 2026
item.description: Meta updated WhatsApp Business API per-message rates for four markets effective April 1, 2026. Pakistan saw the steepest increase: authentication and utility rates rose from $0.0054 to $0.01 (~85%). Saudi Arabia marketing rates increased ~10% to $0.0501. India's authentication-international rate rose ~8.6% to $0.0304. Turkey received the largest cut, with authentication and utility rates falling from $0.0053 to $0.0009 (~83% decrease). Eight new billing currencies (ARS, CLP, COP, MYR, PEN, SAR, SGD, AED) were also introduced on this date.

item.title: AT&T raises A2P SMS +$0.0005/msg (~17%) and MMS +$0.0015/msg (~20%) across all routes
item.description: AT&T increased A2P pass-through fees effective April 1, 2026, reflected on May 2026 invoices. SMS MT and MO rates rose $0.0005 per message (~16.7% increase); MMS MT and MO rates rose $0.0015 per message (~20% increase). Increases apply uniformly across 10DLC, standard-rated short code, and toll-free messaging channels. This follows similar fee hikes from T-Mobile and US Cellular that took effect January 19, 2026.

item.title: T-Mobile and US Cellular raised US A2P SMS carrier fees
item.description: Effective 19 January 2026, T-Mobile increased outbound A2P SMS carrier pass-through fees across long code, toll-free and short code, and US Cellular applied its own A2P pass-through fee updates the same day. These are carrier surcharges added on top of a provider's listed per-message rate, so they raise real US delivery cost without changing headline prices.

item.title: US A2P 10DLC registration fees increased (TCR, August 2025)
item.description: In August 2025 several Campaign Registry (TCR) charges for US A2P 10DLC rose, including brand registration, standard brand vetting and campaign verification fees. These one-time and monthly registration costs are separate from per-message carrier surcharges and must be paid before US A2P SMS can be sent.

item.title: WhatsApp Business moved to per-message pricing (conversation pricing deprecated)
item.description: On 1 July 2025 Meta replaced conversation-based pricing with per-message pricing: each delivered template message is billed individually by category (marketing, utility, authentication) and recipient country, instead of one fee per 24-hour conversation window. Non-template messages sent within an open customer-service window remain free.
